articles.title: THE RETENTION FRAMEWORK THAT GREW REAL PRODUCTS
articles.summary: Social media tactics. Drip campaigns. Push notifications.

articles.title: Instead of random tactics, you should start with a solid framework
articles.summary: that covers the entire user lifecycle—which is exactly what you'll find in this 90+ page ebook by Amplitude [4]:

articles.title: WHY SHOULD I WORRY ABOUT WHAT'S HAPPENING IN THE ECONOMY?
articles.summary: Product leaders must understand the global economy, as it influences planning and risk management. Economic indicators offer insights into market trends, while recognizing the effects of recessions on B2B SaaS - such as slowing sales and increased churn - is essential for adapting strategies and demonstrating ROI.

articles.title: OPENAI'S CPO ON HOW AI CHANGES MUST-HAVE SKILLS, MOATS, CODING, STARTUP PLAYBOOKS, AND MORE (90 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: This post covers OpenAI's agile, bottom-up approach to product development, emphasizing "model maximalism" and the iterative deployment of AI. It underscores the increasing importance of writing effective AI evaluation tests and the lasting value of chat interfaces while predicting the rise of fine-tuned models and the emergence of "vibe coding" within product teams.

articles.title: ESCAPE OKR THEATRE
articles.summary: Key traits of effective OKRs include focus, measurability, realism, cohesion, and alignment with strategy, rather than rigid cascading structures. The framework warns against common pitfalls, such as overloading objectives, relying solely on lagging indicators, or setting vague and obvious goals - advocating instead for clear, strategic OKRs that foster direction, learning, and meaningful progress.

articles.title: WHY YOUR USERS DON'T CARE ABOUT YOUR PRODUCT STRATEGY
articles.summary: Product strategy should be treated as a product itself by clearly defining its audience, the problem it solves, and how success is measured - focusing on enabling teams and stakeholders to make intentional decisions. Rather than simply completing templates, the value lies in aligning team efforts with strategic priorities and confidently rejecting misaligned opportunities.

articles.title: HOW TO AVOID MASSIVE BLOWS WHEN RELEASING NEW FEATURES
articles.summary: Smoke testing is crucial for ensuring new software features don't break existing core functionalities, preventing customer dissatisfaction and trust erosion. Starting with manual testing to define core components and then automating the process within the build pipeline is recommended to maintain product quality and avoid costly errors.

articles.title: DESIGNING SNAPCHAT AND THE FUTURE OF MUSIC (61 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: In this video, Jack Brody, former Head of Product at Snap and current CPO at Suno dives into designing in the age of AI and what the future of music looks like. Brody explores how AI is flipping design on its head and how it has the capacity to change music forever, making music creation more accessible than ever before and empowering newbies and professionals alike.

articles.title: FROM ZERO TO HUNDREDS OF MILLIONS: LESSONS IN COURAGEOUS MARKETING
articles.summary: Udi Ledergor's book, “Courageous Marketing,” champions bold, unconventional B2B strategies, advocating for calculated risks such as regional Super Bowl ads and creative use of offline channels. It emphasizes "punching above your weight" to create the perception of a larger company and driving growth through memorable and impactful campaigns.

articles.title: EVERY MARKETING CHANNEL SUCKS RIGHT NOW
articles.summary: With traditional marketing channels saturated, startups should focus on little channels for higher engagement and novelty, leveraging new technologies and unique product aspects. While product quality is key, innovative and initially unscalable marketing strategies can drive significant early growth.

articles.title: GO TAKE RISKS
articles.summary: Asymmetric risk is where rewards greatly outweigh downsides.

articles.title: WHAT INVESTING AT 100X ARR REALLY MEANS
articles.summary: Raising capital at 100x ARR risks unrealistic revenue growth, setting operators up for failure despite building great companies.
